mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent quarter-over-quarter during late-cycle expansions confirms robust manufacturing output, generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth turns negative during late-cycle phases, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when growth exceeds two percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth signals expanding corporate earnings fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4 percent force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than sixty percent of months where CPIAUCSL monthly change exceeds zero point four percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, driving positive short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 5-day returns in over fifty-five percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment signals a synchronized supply and labor contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in INDPRO and rises in UNRATE fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with accuracy below fifty percent.
